data.title: Canada's Maple Leaf Foods (MFI) Raises Dividend by 10%
data.description:
Canada's Maple Leaf Foods (TSE:MFI) has increased its quarterly dividend by 10%, sending its stock higher as a result.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Toronto-based meat producer said that its board of directors has approve a 10% increase to its quarterly dividend for 2026. That lifts the distribution to $0.21 per share from $0.19 previously. The annual distribution from Maple Leaf Foods will now be $0.84 a share.

The dividend increase applies to Maple Leaf Foods' shareholders of record at the close of business on March 9 of this year. The company is known for its deli meats, bacon and poultry, as well as the popular Schneider's hot dog brand.

Maple Leaf Foods' Strong Outlook

Management at Maple Leaf Foods said the dividend increase is made possible by its strong financial outlook for the year ahead. The company expects mid-single digit sales growth this year, which should help improve both its top and bottom line results.

"Following a year of transformational financial performance, we are increasing our annual dividend by 10 per cent, marking our eleventh consecutive annual increase," said CEO Curtis Frank in a news release. The company recently sold off its pork business. MFI stock has risen 20% over the past 12 months.

Is MFI Stock a Buy?

Maple Leaf Foods' stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among six Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on six Buy ratings issued in the last three months. The average MFI price target of $34.33 implies 29.46% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Wins Twice at the Euro NCAP Best in Class Awards
data.description:
EV maker Tesla (TSLA) recently received two wins at the Euro NCAP Best in Class awards. Indeed, the Model 3 was named the safest Large Family Car, while the Model Y was named the safest Small SUV. These awards are based on how well vehicles perform in crash tests and safety evaluations compared with others in their categories. The achievement was shared by Tesla Europe & Middle East on social media platform X.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to Euro NCAP, the Best in Class awards are calculated using a weighted score across four areas:

  1. Adult Occupant
  2. Child Occupant
  3. Vulnerable Road User
  4. Safety Assist

Only cars that earn a full five-star rating and are tested with standard safety equipment qualify. Interestingly, the updated Model 3 stood out for its child protection and driver-assistance system, while the Model Y also scored highly in those same areas. Euro NCAP even called the Model Y the "gold standard" among small SUVs.

In addition, Euro NCAP Secretary General Dr. Michiel van Ratingen noted that 2025 was the organization's busiest year ever, with more cars tested due to the quick growth of electric vehicles and advanced safety technology. While the Mercedes-Benz CLA (MBGAF) was named the overall Best Performer, Tesla finished only slightly behind.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and eight Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $394.12 per share implies 9.8% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: BMO Capital Markets Pounds the Table on Eli Lilly Stock (LLY)
data.description:
BMO Capital Markets (BMO) is pounding the table on Eli Lilly's (LLY) stock to begin the new year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The brokerage firm has reiterated a Buy-equivalent outperform rating on LLY stock with a $1,200.00 price target, which is 12% higher than where the shares currently sit. BMO says it remains confident in Eli Lilly's global position in the weight-loss drug market and expects the pharma giant to lead in obesity treatments for many years to come.

BMO also highlighted that growth for Eli Lilly's weight-loss business is supported by broadening access, the upcoming launch of its weight-loss pill called Orforglipron, and the company's expanding pipeline of new medications to treat conditions ranging from arthritis to Alzheimer's disease.

Eli Lilly's Leadership in Weight-Loss Drugs

In a note to clients, BMO said that it expects Eli Lilly to maintain its worldwide leadership position in the weight-loss drug space despite rival Novo Nordisk (NVO) beating it to market with a weight-loss pill. Eli Lilly is currently ramping up production of its weight-loss pill in anticipation of a summer 2026 launch.

The brokerage also noted that Eli Lilly has on deck a highly anticipated new drug to help treat Alzheimer's disease and lessen its impact on people. BMO says that results from Eli Lilly's Alzheimer's medication are eagerly awaited and that the company could have yet another blockbuster drug on its hands.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 21 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 19 Buy and two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,192.17 implies 11.52% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Delta Air Stock Resilience Tested as CEO Calls Out Trump's Harmful Rate Cap
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (DAL) CEO Ed Bastian is speaking out against a new proposal to slash credit card interest rates, cautioning that the move could inadvertently hurt the very people it is designed to help. During a Wednesday interview on Bloomberg Television, Bastian addressed the fallout of a potential 10% cap on credit card interest, a policy recently advocated by the Trump administration to provide relief to Americans struggling with high-interest debt.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why the CEO is Sounding the Alarm

To understand why an airline CEO is so concerned about credit cards, we have to look at Delta's bottom line. In 2025, Delta's partnership with American Express (AXP) grew to a staggering $8.2 billion in remuneration. Airlines now earn a huge portion of their profits not from selling tickets, but from selling miles to banks.

Bastian's critique of the 10% cap centers on the second-order effects that politicians often ignore. He stated that "The knock-on effects are pretty significant when you think about what seems to be a good idea to help lower-income strata and bring some interest-rate relief." From an economic perspective, if banks are forced to cap rates at 10%, well below the current average of roughly 21%, they may stop lending to "subprime" or "near-prime" borrowers altogether to avoid losing money.

Delta Aims to Protect the Premium Moat

Interestingly, Bastian noted that Delta's core business might be slightly insulated compared to budget competitors. Because Delta works with American Express, he explained that "that's not an income level that we're focused on in our business." Delta's well-heeled flyers typically have high credit scores and pay their balances in full every month, meaning they aren't usually paying interest anyway.

However, the risk to Delta Air stock lies in the rewards ecosystem. Experts warn that if banks lose billions in interest income, they will likely cut back on the points and miles they give out to attract customers. This could make co-branded cards less attractive, potentially stalling the double-digit growth Delta has seen in its loyalty business.

Watch Out for the January 20, 2026 Deadline

The industry is now staring at a January 20, 2026 deadline set by the administration for compliance. While Delta Air stock saw a slight dip following management's cautious 2026 forecast, the company still expects 20% earnings growth this year. The big question for 2026 is whether the legal pushback from CEOs like Bastian will be enough to delay or modify the interest cap before it fundamentally changes how Americans travel and spend.

Is Delta Air Lines Stock a Good Buy?

On TipRanks, DAL stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 19 unanimous Buy ratings. The average Delta Air Lines price target of $82.11 implies 19.6% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, DAL shares have gained 4.6%.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Trump Tariff Case Won't Receive a Supreme Court Ruling on Wednesday
data.description:
The Supreme Court issued three ruling on Wednesday, but didn't provide a decision on the legality of President Trump's tariffs in its second opinion day of the year. SCOTUS hasn't announced the date for its next opinion day, although it could come as early as next Tuesday, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Airbus Tests Quantum Computing to Improve Aircraft Design
data.description:
PsiQuantum is working with Airbus SE (EADSY) on ways to use quantum computers in aircraft design. The focus is on improving computer models of airflow around aircraft. Today, these models take a lot of time and power to run. Consequently, gains in speed could affect cost and design choices. As a result, the work aims to identify where quantum tools could be most effective in the future.

data.title: Honeywell-Owned (HON) Quantinuum Announces IPO
data.description:
Quantinuum, the quantum computing firm that is majority-owned by Honeywell International (HON) has announced plans to hold an initial public offering (IPO). is Investors may get a chance to buy in soon.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Quantinuum, which is widely regarded as one of the most advanced quantum computing companies, says it is submitting the paper work and registration needed to go public with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

The timing, number of shares to be issued, and price of the IPO have not yet been set. However, the IPO fulfills a pledge made by Honeywell to take the quantum computing firm public sometime in 2026 or 2027, at the latest.

About Quantinuum and Its Business

Quantinuum was founded in 2021 through the merger of Honeywell Quantum Solutions and Cambridge Quantum, a U.K.-based start-up company. The company makes ultra-powerful computers that are capable of solving complex problems and calculations.

Quantinuum competes in the quantum computing space against rivals such as D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Rigetti Computing (RGTI), and IonQ (IONQ). Quantinuum boasts clients such as JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and Softbank (SFTBY). Late last year, chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) invested in Quantinuum through a $600 million funding round.

Is HON Stock a Buy?
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: U.S. Existing Home Sales Surge to 34-Month High as Mortgage Rates Ease
data.description:
Sales of previously owned homes in the U.S. in December grew by 5.1% in December to an annualized rate of 4.35 million homes, said the National Association of Realtors (NAR) on Wednesday. While existing home sales remained at a 30-year low in 2025, December home sales growth was the highest since February 2023.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"2025 was another tough year for homebuyers, marked by record-high home prices and historically low home sales," said NAR Chief Economist Lawrence Yun. "However, in the fourth quarter, conditions began improving, with lower mortgage rates and slower home price growth."

data.title: AMD Stock Slips Despite New Ryzen AI MAX+ 392 Benchmark Data
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock was down on Wednesday after the first benchmark for the upcoming Ryzen AI MAX+ 392 processor appeared on a benchmark test. The latest benchmark test was impressive, with the Ryzen AI MAX+ 392 scoring 2,917 points on the single-core test and scoring 18,071 points in the multi-core test.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Those scores are impressive when investors consider the target audience of the Ryzen AI MAX+ 392 processor. This new chip is designed for use in mobile applications, such as gaming laptops. It also comes integrated with AMD's Radeon 8060S GPUs, allowing it to offer respectable gaming performance on a budget.

AMD's ability to present gaming devices for a fair price is important in the current artificial intelligence (AI) landscape. The demand created by the AI boom has caused the price of computer components such as GPUs and RAM to soar. This has made it difficult for gamers to acquire devices at a decent price, which means AMD can reach that audience with its budget offerings, allowing it to tap a growing market as inflation continues to weigh on personal spending.

AMD Stock Movement Today

AMD stock was down 1.86% on Wednesday but was still up 1.26% year-to-date. The stock has also rallied 84.2% over the past 12 months, largely due to the increased demand for its server components during the AI boom.

AMD stock has also won the praise of analysts recently, including an upgrade to a Buy rating from five-star KeyBanc analyst John Vinh. The analyst also set a price target of $270 for AMD stock, suggesting a 24.88% possible upside.
data.author: William White
